Title: pHBMT1, a BAHD-family monolignol acyltransferase, mediates lignin acylation in poplar

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: developing xylem cell wall lignin; methanol-soluble xylem fraction
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BAHD acyltransferase family; Pfam PF02458 CoA-dependent transferase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers the p-hydroxybenzoyl group from p-hydroxybenzoyl-CoA to monolignol alcohols, preferentially sinapyl alcohol and also coniferyl alcohol, to form monolignol–p-hydroxybenzoate conjugates.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: branch point / monolignol conjugation step before lignin polymerization
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms lignin-incorporable monolignol–p-hydroxybenzoate conjugates that increase pHB acylation of lignin
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step supported, but rate-limiting not formally established
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest in developing xylem/internode tissue; low or absent in mature leaf; positively correlated with xylem pHB levels across natural genotypes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpressed in developing xylem of transgenic P39 lines
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in vitro enzyme screening of 116 BAHD proteins via wheat-germ cell-free translation; LC-MS enzyme assays; enzyme kinetics; correlation of transcript abundance with natural pHB variation; transgenic overexpression in poplar; HPLC after saponification; DFRC; 2D-NMR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases soluble and cell-wall-bound p-hydroxybenzoate and increases lignin-incorporated S-pHB in transgenic poplar without altering total lignin content or biomass growth.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: field-grown natural P. trichocarpa genotypes for correlation analysis; greenhouse-grown 4-month-old transgenic poplars for functional validation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No detectable penalty on height, stem diameter, total lignin content, S/G ratio, or major lignin interunit linkage proportions under the tested conditions; excess product may be diverted to soluble/vacuolar detoxified forms.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of developing xylem from natural P. trichocarpa genotypes; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: pHBMT1 was the only one of five in vitro-active candidates whose expression significantly correlated with pHB content. It showed strong preference for sinapyl alcohol over coniferyl alcohol and no detectable activity with p-coumaryl alcohol. Some overexpression lines accumulated more soluble than lignin-bound pHB, suggesting possible glycosylation/sequestration of excess conjugates.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. pHBMT1 was identified from a BAHD-family screen as the most selective enzyme using p-hydroxybenzoyl-CoA with monolignols. 2. Its transcript abundance in developing xylem positively correlated with natural variation in cell-wall-bound pHB across P. trichocarpa genotypes. 3. Overexpression in hybrid poplar increased soluble pHBA, cell-wall-bound pHB, and lignin-incorporated S-pHB. 4. Biomass growth and total lignin amount were not significantly changed under tested conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to engineer lignocellulosic biomass with elevated releasable p-hydroxybenzoate for lignin-first biorefinery applications; may be combined with strategies that increase p-hydroxybenzoyl-CoA precursor supply to further enhance pHB output.
